summary: High‑energy second set with killer Estimated > He's Gone > Eyes run
blurb: The band rolls through a blistering Alabama Getaway into Greatest Story Ever Told, then launches an unstoppable second set featuring seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and the iconic Estimated Prophet → He's Gone → Eyes sequence. Jerry’s soaring solos and Brent’s bright keys keep the momentum rolling all night.
review: December 12, 1980 at Swing Auditorium captured a Grateful Dead that were truly on fire. The first set opened with a ripping Alabama Getaway that segued into a heartfelt Greatest Story Ever Told, while Jerry Garcia delivered emotive, “cooking” solos on both tracks and later lit up Friend Of The Devil and Althea. The second set is the show’s crown jewel: a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition gave way to an unforgettable Estimated Prophet → He's Gone → Eyes of the World run that had the entire house in awe. Brent Mydland’s keyboards added bright, lilting touches throughout, especially on Wharf Rat and Friend Of The Devil, while Phil Lesh’s bass “bombs” drove the jams forward. Bob Weir’s slide work shone on Althea and the Truckin' coda, and the twin‑drum attack from Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann kept a tight, propulsive pulse. Across all available tapes the performance is consistently praised as tight, high‑octane, and emotionally resonant – a must‑listen for anyone chasing that early‑’80s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are marked positive; multiple reviewers repeatedly highlight the same standout moments (Estimated > He's Gone > Eyes, China Cat → Rider, Alabama/Getaway). No negative comments about performance appear, indicating strong consensus of excellence across the show.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with the clearest mix despite minor bass distortion; captures the full band balance and is the community’s default reference.

key_highlights: Estimated Prophet → He's Gone → Eyes of the World sequence stands out as a peak moment.
Blistering Alabama Getaway into Greatest Story Ever Told kicks off the night with high voltage.
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue fuels the second‑set momentum.
Brent’s bright keyboards and tight twin‑drum work add depth to the high‑octane jams.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring, emotive solos throughout – from the cooking Greatest Story Ever Told to monster leads on Alabama, Friend Of The Devil and the heartfelt He's Gone.
band_performance.Phil: Bass “bombs” drive the jams, especially during the Estimated > He's Gone > Eyes run and on high‑energy tracks like Althea and Truckin'.
band_performance.Bob: Slide work shines on Althea, Little Red Rooster and the extended Truckin' coda; provides solid rhythm and vocal support throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Adds bright, lilting keyboard solos on Friend Of The Devil and majestic B3 textures in Wharf Rat; his early‑80s tone lifts the entire set.
